questions.text: Which of these is an abiotic component of environment?
questions.explanation: Soil is an abiotic (non-living) component. Plants, animals, and bacteria are biotic (living) components.

questions.text: Energy flow in an ecosystem is unidirectional.
questions.explanation: Energy flows in one direction in an ecosystem: from sun → producers → consumers → decomposers. It does not cycle back.

questions.correctAnswer: The increase in concentration of harmful substances at each trophic level.
questions.explanation: Biological magnification is the accumulation of harmful chemicals (like pesticides) in increasing concentration at each trophic level.

questions.text: The functional unit of environment is ________.
questions.explanation: An ecosystem is the functional unit of environment where living organisms interact with each other and with their physical environment.
